description: Local-only PR #646 manual capture prep packet for qualitative plain-vs-Alpha review; capture only, with no scoring, ranking, blinding, source maps, provider calls, local model calls, readiness claims, benchmark claims, or Alpha-superiority claims.

cases.prompt: In the Alpha Solver repo after PR #646, PR #647, and PR #648, identify the next answer-quality bottleneck that should be addressed before any broader evaluation lane. Ground the answer in alpha_solver_portable.py, tests/test_alpha_substantive_lift_contract.py, tests/test_alpha_local_runtime_honesty.py, and docs/OPERATOR_RUN_CAPTURE.md. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets the post-#646/#647/#648 bottleneck question while asking for the six PR #646 substantive-lift moves. Capture only; do not score or rank outputs.

cases.prompt: Decide whether Alpha Solver should next wire runtime lift behavior into /v1/solve or keep testing PR #646 manually through alpha_solver_portable.py and the operator capture harness. Use alpha/eval/operator_run_capture.py, scripts/operator_run_capture.py, alpha_solver_portable.py, and .specs/ALPHA-SOLVER-SUBSTANTIVE-LIFT-ANSWER-CONTRACT-001.md as constraints. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets runtime lift wiring versus manual portable testing without changing provider/runtime behavior.

cases.prompt: Recommend how to investigate whether route/persona metadata helps answer quality or merely adds bloat in Alpha Solver, without activating new routes or changing persona behavior. Ground the recommendation in docs/OPERATOR_RUN_CAPTURE.md, alpha/eval/operator_run_capture.py, and the existing route-metadata boundary. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets route/persona causality versus bloat while preserving the capture-harness boundary.

cases.prompt: Assess the safest next repo task for local ToT honesty after PR #647 and PR #648, given that unsupported local synthesis must SAFE-OUT rather than fabricate or echo templates. Ground the answer in tests/test_alpha_local_runtime_honesty.py, tests/test_safeout_confidence_honesty.py if present, and alpha_solver_portable.py. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets local ToT honesty and unsupported synthesis boundary.

cases.prompt: Design a manual evaluation workflow for checking whether PR #646 creates an operator-visible answer-quality difference, but do not score, rank, blind, unblind, or claim superiority. Ground the plan in docs/OPERATOR_RUN_CAPTURE.md, scripts/operator_run_capture.py, and alpha/eval/operator_run_capture.py. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets manual evaluation design while preserving all non-scoring and non-claim boundaries.

cases.prompt: Choose exactly one constrained implementation lane Alpha Solver should do after this manual-eval prep packet: strengthen portable prompt wording, add more local structural checks, improve capture documentation, or defer implementation until qualitative review. Ground the choice in .specs/INDEX.md, .specs/ALPHA-SOLVER-SUBSTANTIVE-LIFT-ANSWER-CONTRACT-001.md, tests/test_alpha_substantive_lift_contract.py, and docs/OPERATOR_RUN_CAPTURE.md. Include intent diagnosis, hidden assumption, dominant tradeoff, committed recommendation, failure condition, and same-day next action.
cases.notes: Targets a constrained implementation-lane selection without starting the lane.
